title: PackUK's 2027 recyclability method gives paper packaging buyers an early data deadline
summary: PackUK has published its updated Recyclability Assessment Methodology for packaging supplied in 2027, clarifying material rules, definitions and the evidence used to decide whether packaging is commonly collected. The red, amber and green ratings will feed into UK packaging EPR fee modulation, while 2025 and 2026 reporting continues under the earlier method.
buyerTakeaway: Map each bakery and foodservice paper SKU to the correct RAM version, especially coated paperboard and fiber-based composites, and collect construction and recyclability evidence early enough to model 2027 pEPR costs before specifications are locked.
imageAlt: Recycling containers shown with Packaging Insights' report on PackUK's 2027 recyclability methodology

title: Seaweed barrier coating trial puts scale and recyclability under scrutiny
summary: Amcor and Kelpi are evaluating a seaweed-based barrier coating for fiber packaging, with the program focused on commercial viability, scalability, protection, high-speed processability and compatibility with paper recycling streams. The work reflects growing demand for renewable barriers that can replace harder-to-recycle fossil-derived coatings without weakening pack performance.
buyerTakeaway: For bakery and foodservice paper trials, require application-specific barrier data, converting-line results, food-contact documentation and recycling evidence at the intended coat weight before treating a bio-based coating as production-ready.
imageAlt: Amcor fiber packaging range shown in Packaging Insights' report on the Kelpi seaweed coating trial

title: New organic-fluorine screening method sharpens PPWR evidence planning
summary: Norner has introduced a Total Organic Fluorine screening method using pyrolysis-GC/MS to distinguish PFAS-relevant organic fluorine from inorganic fluorine in solid materials. The method is positioned for coatings, composites and products with uncertain PFAS histories, with targeted PFAS analysis added where the screening result or risk profile requires it.
buyerTakeaway: For EU-bound grease-resistant paper and coated food packaging, agree the screening method, reporting limit, sample construction and escalation to targeted PFAS testing before production; a generic 'PFAS-free' statement is not a substitute for finished-item evidence.
imageAlt: Laboratory testing image published with Packaging Insights' report on PFAS screening

title: Official guidance puts the EU and Northern Ireland BPA deadline into buyer terms
summary: Food Standards Scotland says most newly manufactured or imported food-contact materials first placed on the EU or Northern Ireland market from 20 July 2026 must meet the EU restrictions on intentionally used BPA and other hazardous bisphenols, subject to defined transitions. It also distinguishes this requirement from the current position in Great Britain.
buyerTakeaway: For coated, printed or bonded bakery paper supplied to the EU or Northern Ireland, confirm market-placement dates and obtain current supplier assurances, declarations and supporting purity or test data; do not treat a GB-only compliance position as sufficient.

title: India food authority warning highlights migration risks from non-food-grade printing
summary: India's food safety authority has again directed food businesses to stop using newspapers to pack or serve food, citing possible migration of printing-ink components and contaminants. The warning is a practical reminder that printed paper must be designed and documented for its intended food-contact use.
buyerTakeaway: For printed liners, wraps and cake cups, specify food-contact inks, low odor, controlled set-off and migration support instead of accepting general-purpose printed paper.
sourceName: Press Information Bureau, Government of India

title: Potato-starch barriers target PFAS-free grease resistance for bakery paper
summary: Royal Avebe presented its plant-based barrier systems for fiber food packaging at Barrier Coatings for Food Packaging 2026. PerfectaFILM is designed to limit oil and grease penetration in paper, while PerfectaCOL combines heat-pressure sealing with barrier performance for dry foods, baked goods and foodservice formats.
buyerTakeaway: Ask coating suppliers for finished-paper grease resistance, food-contact status, BfR/FDA support, coat-weight targets and sealing trials on the actual base paper; a bio-based ingredient claim alone does not prove production performance or finished-pack compliance.
imageAlt: Royal Avebe image for its plant-based barrier coatings for food packaging

title: Taller cupcake liners emerge as a simple bakery presentation upgrade
summary: Consumer baking coverage is drawing attention to taller paper liners as a low-cost way to improve cupcake height, shape and shelf presence. For commercial buyers, the trend turns small dimensional differences into a visible part of the finished product.
buyerTakeaway: Specify wall height, base diameter, paper weight, rigidity and pan fit on the purchase order; a generic 'standard' size is not enough for consistent presentation.
imageAlt: Taller paper cupcake liners shown in Good Housekeeping's baking report

title: European Commission simplifies EUDR implementation while keeping buyer traceability relevant
summary: The Commission's 2026 EUDR review packages updated guidance, FAQs, product-scope work and information-system changes. The simplification effort is expected to reduce compliance costs, but paper and packaging buyers still need clear product-scope decisions and dependable supplier-origin records.
buyerTakeaway: For EU orders, record whether each paper packaging SKU is in scope and retain supplier, fibre-origin and due-diligence references where required instead of relying on a generic sustainability certificate.

title: BfR draft consolidates food-contact paper recommendations and sharpens substance identification
summary: Germany's BfR is consulting on a consolidated, tabular revision of Recommendations XXXVI, XXXVI/1, XXXVI/2 and XXXVI/3 for food-contact paper and board. The draft adds CAS-number precision, which can affect how raw materials are matched to compliant entries.
buyerTakeaway: For LFGB/BfR projects, match the final paper grade, additives and coating to the applicable recommendation and confirm that supplier substance identifiers align with the revised entries.
sourceName: German Federal Institute for Risk Assessment (BfR)

title: Air fryer paper liner outlook keeps sizing, heat safety and private label in focus
summary: A 2026 market outlook links continued paper-liner demand to air-fryer adoption, convenience cooking and commercial-kitchen use. Product development is concentrating on basket fit, grease resistance, heat performance, compostability claims and retail-ready private-label formats.
buyerTakeaway: Approve liner shape, dimensions, perforation, heat rating, oil resistance, warning copy and carton configuration together before placing a volume order.
imageAlt: Air fryer paper liners market image published by IndexBox
